{{Listen|filename=SimonWarCry.flac|title=Simon speaks|description=Simon using the Ewokese language}}
'''Simon''', known as '''Simon the Killer Ewok''', was an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] with [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]] and black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Simon wielded a [[Staff/Legends|staff]] weapon that fired devastating [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolts capable of quickly destroying buildings, [[Species/Legends|beings]], [[Droid/Legends|droids]], and vehicles. Simon was also equipped with an [[Personal energy shield/Legends|energy shield]] and was capable of moving at fast speed.<ref name="GB" /> Simon stood at one [[Meter/Legends|meter]] tall,<ref name="Databank" /> wore a piece of orange hooded [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] that covered their chest and head, with holes for their [[Ear/Legends|ears]] and face. The Ewok also wore a headpiece with two plumes and spoke the [[Ewokese/Legends|Ewokese]] [[Language/Legends|language]].<ref name="GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Simon the Killer Ewok is about as canon as the hyperkinetic rabbity thing, Max.|Leland Chee|<ref name="Chee">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=0960#15884843|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-11-04|time=12:34 AM|quote=Simon the Killer Ewok is about as canon as the hyperkinetic rabbity thing, Max.}}</ref>}}
Simon the Killer Ewok appeared in the [[2001]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', where they could be accessed by typing the cheat code "Simonsays" in the game's chat box. After the code is entered, Simon would appear on any stretch of land that the player is overlooking. The color of the plumes on Simon's headpiece are dependent on the team color of the player the Ewok is summoned by. They can be blue, red, green, yellow, purple, gray, orange, or cyan. Simon's model and profile image are identical to that of [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]], who appears in multiple campaigns in the game.<ref name="GB" />

Unlike most of the game's other cheat codes, "Simonsays" was not mentioned in [[Prima Games]]' [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] for ''Galactic Battlegrounds'',<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> and was instead revealed on the LucasArts website.<ref name="Lucas">{{LucasArtsCite|url=products/battlegrounds/hintstips.htm|text=Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds - Hints and Tips}}</ref> ''Galactic Battlegrounds'' level designer [[Lily Mansfield]] believed that Simon was named in reference to [[Simon Jeffrey]], the president of LucasArts at the time.<ref name="Interview">{{WookYouTube|J1w3DCX2H7E|A Conversation with Lily Mansfield|old=1}}</ref> When asked about Simon's [[canon]]icity on the [[StarWars.com Message Boards]], [[Lucasfilm Ltd.|Lucasfilm's]] [[Holocron continuity database]] manager [[Leland Chee]] compared Simon to [[Max (bunny)|Max]],<ref name="Chee" /> a character who appeared as an [[Easter egg (virtual)|Easter egg]] in the [[1997]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''.<ref name="Dark">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''</ref>
